public_claim: For years, the same restaurant delivered to this building with no apartment number and explicit instructions to leave the order in the lobby. I never personally identified a resident as R., so the address proves a routine—not a person.

private_messages.message: Manny, you’re the only person I clearly remember handling those lobby orders. I need us precise: what nights did you collect them, and did you ever hand one directly to anyone claiming to be R.?

Rafi, our accounts of lobby traffic may differ. Before either of us makes categorical claims, let’s separate people we actually recognized from people merely buzzed through. I won’t speculate about identities if you don’t.

ability: Use two decades of building knowledge to distinguish verified residents and routines from anonymous lobby traffic.
goal_progress: Publicly framed the building connection as a delivery routine rather than proof that R. lived there.
Asked Manny for direct corroboration about who handled the orders.
Opened a channel with Rafi to prevent contradictory overclaims without mentioning the footage gap.
reasoning_summary: The strongest safe facts concern the anonymous lobby-drop routine. Coordinating first with Manny and Rafi protects my account while avoiding premature disclosure of EV10. The absence of a verified resident favors an institutional construct, but disappearance alone does not establish a crime or victim identity.
